full.title: Version
full.description: Schema version for the venvironment.yaml configuration file. Acceptance criteria: equal major version, less/equal minor and patch version.

full.title: Application Models
full.description: List of application models representing some program. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: CAN and CAN FD Networks
full.description: List of CAN and CAN FD networks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: CAN Replay Blocks
full.description: List of CAN replay blocks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: CarMaker interface
full.description: CarMaker interface. Entry can be deactivated with when.

quantities-file-path.description: Absolute or relative path to a .vcdl file that defines the selected CarMaker quantities (system variables) and their grouping. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

host.description: The host name or the IPv4 address of the system on which CarMaker is running. If the input is incorrect, no connection will be established.

user.description: The name of the user running CarMaker (Windows or Linux username). Used to select one of multiple CarMaker instances. If an empty string is specified, the first CarMaker instance will be used. If set to "localuser", the local username must match the one on the CarMaker host.

backlog-size.description: Number of incoming data vectors which should be buffered.

polling-cycle-ms.description: Number of milliseconds between two automatic poll calls. If set to 0, automatic polling is disabled and polling must be performed via CAPL code.

full.title: Databases
full.description: List of databases. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Datasources
full.description: List of datasources. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Defines
full.description: List of defines to be passed to CAPL, .NET, Python and VCDL. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Diagnostic description
full.description: Diagnostic description with CDD.

full.title: EtherCAT settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.
full.description: Configure EtherCAT.

full.title: CAN Networks
full.description: List of Ethernet networks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Ethernet Replay Blocks
full.description: List of Ethernet replay blocks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: FDX
full.description: FDX settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.

transport-layer.description: Protocol to be used as transport layer for the FDX protocol

port.description: UDP or TCP port to be used for the FDX protocol

description-files.description: List of FDX description files (.xml) that describe which signals or variables are to be exchanged and the manner in which this is to occur (e.g., with regard to grouping).

full.title: FlexRay Clusters
full.description: List of FlexRay clusters. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: FlexRay Replay Blocks
full.description: List of FlexRay replay blocks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Functional mockup units
full.description: List of functional mockup units. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Global Settings
full.description: Global settings for all scenarios.

full.title: Include files
full.description: Include a different file (similar to #include of the c preprocessor). The order of includes does not matter. Circular/multiple includes are resolved correctly.

full.title: LIN Networks
full.description: List of LIN networks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: LIN Replay Blocks
full.description: List of LIN replay blocks. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Logging Block
full.description: Configuration of the logging.

full.title: Programming Modules
full.description: Lists of programming modules to be used by the simulation. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Scenarios
full.description: List of scenarios with the option to define or override variables and defines.

full.title: Security
full.description: Security settings

operating-system-stack-profile-id.description: The ID of the assigned security profile to the OS Ethernet stack.

shared-canoe-stack-profile-id.description: The ID of the assigned security profile to the shared CANoe Ethernet stack.

test-stack-profile-id.description: The ID of the assigned security profile to the test Ethernet stack.

full.title: Sensor configuration
full.description: Sensor configuration. Entry can be deactivated with when.

full.title: SIL Kit
full.description: SIL Kit settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.

participant-name.description: Name used by the simulation tool to join a simulation as a participant at the start of a measurement.

simulation-step-in-micro-sec.description: Time length of a single simulation step. Valid only when "time-source" is set to "external-sil-kit".

life-cycle-event-timeout-in-sec.description: Maximum waiting time for the other simulation participants. Valid only when "time-source" is set to "external-sil-kit".

full.title: Simulation Nodes
full.description: List of simulation nodes. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Symbol mappings
full.description: List of symbol mapping files. Please ensure that the mappings are valid for the environment. No symbol validation will be performed. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: System Variables
full.description: A list of system variable files. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: User Files
full.description: List of user files. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Variables
full.description: List of variables to be used in this configuration file.

full.title: VIO System
full.description: VIO System settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.

ccd-folder.description: Absolute or relative path to the folder containing the Card Capability Description files (.ccd) of the I/O cards. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: VT System
full.description: VT System settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.

module-description-folder.description: Absolute or relative path to the folder containing the VT System module description files. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: XCP files
full.description: List of XCP configuration files. Entries can be deactivated with when.

full.title: XIL API Settings
full.description: Enables the XIL API server. Entry can be deactivated with when.

port.description: TCP port to be used for the XIL API, usually set to "3030" for CANoe

full.title: DYNA4
full.description: DYNA4 settings. Entry can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Application Model
full.description: Describes an application model (.can/.canencr/.cs/.sln/.py/.vmodule/.dll (dotnet assembly)). Also supports local defines.

full.title: CAN or CAN FD network
full.description: A network using the CAN or CAN FD protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this CAN/CAN FD network in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to one or more databases declared in the configuration file. If multiple databases are referenced, the network settings will be read from the first database with valid settings following the order provided here.

application-channel.description: Index of the application channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 255. Value "0" is not allowed.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this CAN/CAN FD network in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to one or more databases declared in the configuration file. If multiple databases are referenced, the network settings will be read from the first database with valid settings following the order provided here.

application-channel.description: Index of the application channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 255. Value "0" is not allowed.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this CAN/CAN FD network in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to one or more databases declared in the configuration file. If multiple databases are referenced, the network settings will be read from the first database with valid settings following the order provided here.

application-channel.description: Index of the application channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 255. Value "0" is not allowed.

full.title: CAN replay block
full.description: A replay block for the CAN protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this CAN replay block in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.description: Node is added to the environment only if the specified condition is true.

oneOf.description: A condition containing logical operators (e.g., ` (intvar > 0 && boolvar) || strvar != "setup_a"`). Variables do not have to be escaped. The additional variable "SCENARIO_NAME" always contains the current scenario name.
oneOf.$comment: impossible to express this grammar as a regex

full.title: Database
full.description: Describes a .arxml/.dbc/.ldf. A network from the database can be selected implicitly via "name" or explicitly via "network-name".

network-name.description: Selects a network from the database (cluster if .arxml). Overrides the selection of the name property.

full.title: Datasources
full.description: Defines datasources used by the application models (.vcdl/.vcodm). Input files can be deactivated with when.

full.title: Define
full.description: Defines to be passed to CAPL, .NET, Python and VCDL.

full.title: Diagnostic description
full.description: Diagnostic description with CDD, MDX, PDX or RDX.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this diagnostic description in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

interface.description: Interfaces in diagnostic description files define the communication parameters for accessing an ECU. Additional default interfaces are offered.

network-assignment.description: Name of the network this diagnostic description is attached to.

full.title: Main devices configuration.
full.description: List of EtherCAT main devices.

full.title: Sub device configuration.
full.description: List of EtherCAT sub devices.

full.title: Ethernet network
full.description: A network using the Ethernet protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this ethernet network in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to a database declared in the configuration file.

global-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.description: Address configuration for the global TCP/IP stack. This setting is relevant if global-settings/ethernet/global-tcp-ip-stack/selected-stack is set to "canoe".

test-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.description: Address configuration for the test TCP/IP stack. This setting is relevant if global-settings/ethernet/test-tcp-ip-stack/selected-stack is set to "individual".

application-channel.description: Index of the application channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 32. Value "0" is not allowed.

measurement-ports.description: A list of measurement ports whose data you want to measure (e.g. for logging).

full.title: Ethernet replay block
full.description: A replay block for the Ethernet protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this ethernet replay block in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a FDX description file (.xml). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: FlexRay cluster
full.description: A cluster using the FlexRay protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this FlexRay cluster in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to a database declared in the configuration file.

application-channel.description: Index of the Application Channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 32. Value "0" is not allowed.

mode.description:
The operation mode of VN Interface. 
"synchronous": In synchronous cc state frames can be sent and received. 
"asynchronous": In all bus phases frames and symbols can be received. Sending is not possible. 
"mixed": In asynchronous cc phase frames and symbols can be received. In synchronous cc state frames can can be sent and received.

automatic-restart.description: Restart of the communication controller when synchronisation is lost.

send-wakeup-pattern.description: Send Wakeup Pattern at measurement start. If true database settings are used

full.title: FlexRay replay block
full.description: A replay block for the FlexRay protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this FlexRay replay block in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

network.description: Name of the FlexRay cluster, this replay node is attached to.

replay-nodes.description: List of nodes, whose transmitted messages should be replayed from the logging file. If not set, all are active by default.

active-model-variables.description: List of FMU model variables to be considered during the simulation.

inactive-model-variables.description: List of FMU model variables to be ignored during the simulation.

namespace-index.description:
If FMUs share the same namespace, this value is used to create a unique namespace by appending a suffix.
The default is no suffix, values above 0 result in "[fmu-name]_[value]".

selected-type.description: FMI3 supports multiple types of models in one FMU while CANoe only supports co-simulation and scheduled-execution. If the selected FMU supports both, one of them has to be selected for simulation.

full.title: Functional mockup unit
full.description: Defines a functional mockup unit (.fmu). By default, all the fmu variables are activated.

full.title: Global settings for Ethernet
full.description: Global settings for Ethernet.

access-mode.description: Mode of the underlying Ethernet bus. Classic approach is the "channel-based" setup. More recent and recommended mode is the switched "network-based" mode (port based).

signal-updates.description: Specifies how signals are updated on Network-based access. Will be ignored on channel-based setups. "always": events of all ports are used to update a signal without qualified Ethernet port. "rx-only": only packets received by the interface are used, which leads to less duplicated updates. "never": signals without qualified Ethernet ports are not updated.

oneOf.description: Events of all ports are used to update a signal without qualified Ethernet port.
Only packets received by the interface are used, which leads to less duplicated updates.
Signals without qualified Ethernet ports are not updated.

test-tcp-ip-stack.description: Configuration for the test TCP/IP stack. If individual configuration a port labeled "TestStack" is created automatically.

full.title: Global settings for FlexRay
full.description: Global settings for FlexRay controlling the interpretation of frames and PDUs of FlexRay configurations with channels A and B.

enable-dual-channel-support.description: If active, PDUs on both channels will be configured with channel mask "AB" and channel postfixes (e.g. xy_Ch_A, xy_Ch_B) will be created for ambiguous objects. If this property is provided "postfixes-for-ambiguous-pdus-on-channel" is forbidden.

postfixes-for-ambiguous-pdus-on-channel.description: Definition of postfix settings if dual channel support is not activated. If this property is provided "enable-dual-channel-support" is forbidden.

full.title: Global settings for database
full.description: Global settings for database.

autosar-pdu-layer.description:
Configures the PDU layer in relation to AUTOSAR databases.
"no-pdus" : Creation of frames only 
"from-autosar-4.0" : Creation of AUTOSAR PDUs for AUTOSAR DBs >= 4.0 
"from-autosar-4.2" : Creation of AUTOSAR PDUs for AUTOSAR DBs >= 4.2

autosar-text-table-entries.description:
Controls naming of text table entries and influences signal qualification.
"compu-const": uses text from COMPU-CONST nodes.
 "short-label": uses text from SHORT-LABEL nodes.

some-ip-pdus-without-service-context.description: Controls the generation of services for SOME/IP PDUs without service context.

use-application-layer-objects.description: Generates application layer objects for the specified network types.

split-overlapping-nm-flexray-frames.description: If active, FlexRay network management frames whose slot cycles overlap are split into several frames. If necessary, new frames/PDUs are created and optimally distributed to the free slot cycles found in the schedule table.

full.title: Global settings for .NET
full.description: Global settings for .NET

database-namespace-generation.description: This setting can be used to specify that certain namespaces should be generated for all .NET symbols (regardless of their uniqueness). The options "use-node" and "use-frame" are only relevant for signals. If no value is given, the namespaces for signals are automatically generated so that each class is unique. For each frame, a class is generated in the namespace "NetworkDB.Frames".

full.title: Global settings for the simulation working mode.
full.description: Global settings for the simulation working mode.

time-scaling-factor.description:
This property is only evaluated if "time-source" is set to "internal-scaled". If a number is provided, the measurement is slowed-down by this factor. For scaling factors between zero and one, the simulation is accelerated accordingly. For example, if you enter the value 0.1 the measurement is accelerated by a factor of 10. 
"as-fast-as-possible": Run the simulation as fast as possible. The simulation speed is not constant and depends on the performance of the computer and the load that is provoked by the simulation.

time-source.description:
Time source for the simulation. 
If this property is set, "default-network-mapping" is also required. 
"internal-realtime": The time response of the measurement (time basis) is controlled internally. 
"internal-scaled": The simulation speed is provided through the property "time-scaling-factor". 
"external-software": The time response of the measurement (time basis) is controlled by an external program which is not Vector SIL Kit. 
"external-sil-kit" : The time response of the measurement (time basis) is controlled by Vector SIL Kit. 
"external-hardware": The time response of the measurement (time basis) is controlled by external hardware.

default-network-mapping.description:
Defines the default network mapping to an underlying layer. 
Connects the networks either to simulated network ("internal-simulator"), hardware ("external-hardware") or to SIL Kit ("external-sil-kit"). 
This value can be overwritten on network level. If "time-source" is set, this property is also required. 
The supported options depend on the time-source. 
"internal-realtime", "external-software" and "external-sil-kit" are supporting "internal-simulator" and "external-sil-kit".
"internal-scaled" only supports "internal-simulator".
"external-hardware" supports all mappings.

full.title: Global settings for Python
full.description: Global settings for Python

interpreter.description: The Python interpreter version. If no interpreter is specified, one is selected automatically. Version greater than or equal to 3.10.0 are supported.

full.title: Global performance settings
full.description: Global performance settings

concurrent-model-execution.description:
Timing behavior of the remaining bus simulation while executing concurrent application models.
 "prioritize-simulation": If an overrun occurs during the concurrent execution of a model, this is reported to the model and the pending model execution is delayed until the previous model execution has been completed. During this delay, the simulation time continues to run.
 If time-scaling-factor as-fast-is-possible is active this behavior does not work, so the behavior "wait-on-overrun" is used instead.
 "wait-on-overrun": If an overrun occurs during the concurrent execution of a model, the simulation time is paused until the previous model execution has been completed. 
 "wait-during-calculation": The simulation time is paused during model execution. The model only runs concurrently with actions that are to be executed at the same simulation time.

full.title: LIN network
full.description: A network using the LIN protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this LIN network in the configuration file or in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database.description: Reference to a database declared in the configuration file.

application-channel.description: Index of the application channel. Each bus type should provide consecutive indices. Starts counting at 1. Value between 1 and 255. Value "0" is not allowed.

mode.description:
Working mode of the LIN interface.
 "commander": the LIN network interface is able to output message headers on the network.
"responder": the LIN network interface responds to received LIN headers if a response to the LIN ID contained within the header has been configured.
 "commander-no-resistor": commander but with disabled resistor.

full.title: LIN replay block
full.description: A replay block for the LIN protocol.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this LIN replay block in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

replay-mode.description:
Selects events to be replayed. 
"all-responses": replay all the responses.
"master-responses-only": replay the responses only for frames published by the Master node.
"master-requests-only": replay only 0x3c responses.
"no-headers": disable replay of the LIN frame headers.

send-events.description:
Selects which events from "rx-responses", "tx-responses" and "wakeup-signals" are replayed. If not set, the default value depends on the value of replay-mode.
 For "all-responses" or "master-responses-only": all events are sent.
 For "master-requests-only": only wakeup-events are sent.
 For "no-headers": none of the events are sent.

full.description:
Name of a .blf or .mf4 frame logging file, supporting the field codes: {ComputerName}, {LocalTime}, {MeasurementIndex}, {MeasurementStart}, {IncSize|001|100MB}, {IncTime|001|01h00m}, {IncTrigger|001}, {TriggerCondition} and {IncTrigger|001}_{TriggerCondition}.
.mf4 is not supported for target architecture Linux64.
List of event types to be logged.
Advanced logging configurations.

warn-overwritten-log-file.description: Specifies whether a warning is shown at the start of measurement before overwriting an existing logging file.

show-error-on-data-loss.description: Specifies whether an error is shown after the measurement if data loss occurred during the measurement. Lines in the logging file marked with a * as a special symbol have corrupted lines around them.

full.description:
Toggle trigger settings. The user can control the start and stop of logging. For example via CAPL.
 Must not be used together with "test-trigger".

pre-trigger-time-ms.description: Time before triggering that should also be logged.

post-trigger-time-ms.description: Time after the triggered stopped that should also be logged.

buffer-size.description: Event buffer size for events pre-trigger-time-ms

full.description: Test trigger settings. The execution period of test elements controls the start and stop of logging.

scope.description: Scope of the test trigger, for each a logging file is created.

verdict.description: List of test verdicts for which logging files will remain stored.

pre-trigger-time-ms.description: Time before triggering that should also be logged.

post-trigger-time-ms.description: Time after the triggered stopped that should also be logged.

buffer-size.description: Event buffer size for events pre-trigger-time-ms

full.title: Programming modules
full.description: Describes a CAPL or C library (.vmodule).

capl-library-path.description: Absolute or relative path to a CAPL library (.vmodule). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

c-library-path.description: Absolute or relative path to a C-library (.vmodule). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: Scenario
full.description: A scenario with the option to define or override variables and defines.

name.description: Name of the scenario

full.title: Network Security Profile Assignments
full.description: List with security profile to network assignments. The security profile is used for SecOC, Diagnostics and Backend Access.

items.title: Network Security Profile Assignment
items.description: Assignment of a security profile to a network.

network.description: The name of the network to which the security profile shall be assigned.

profile-id.description: The ID of the assigned security profile.

full.title: Ethernet Stack Security Profile Assignments
full.description: List with security profile to Ethernet stack assignments. The security profile is used for TLS and IPSec.

items.title: Ethernet Stack Security Profile Assignment
items.description: Assignment of a security profile to an Ethernet stack.

simulation-node.description: The name of the simulation node. The security profile will be assigned to the Ethernet stack of the simulation node.

profile-id.description: The ID of the assigned security profile.
The ID of the assigned security profile to be used for securing Ethernet communication with MACsec. This security profile must contain valid MACsec port configurations.

ports.description: List of physical ports to be protected with MACsec. Each port must be assigned a MACsec port configuration from the security profile.

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a sensor configuration file (.sensor). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: Hybdrid Network Mappings
full.description: Mappings connecting CANoe and SIL Kit networks of type CAN or Ethernet.
Absolute or relative path to a SIL Kit config file (.yaml/.json). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: Simulation Node
full.description: Represents a simulation node.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this simulation node block in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

database-node.description: Assigns explicitly a database node to a simulation node. If not set, the simulation-node name will be used as database-node. To deactivate automatic assignment, it should be set to false. For CAN networks, if the node is contained in more than one database assigned to the network, the name of the database should be specified using the syntax "dbName::nodeName".

modeling-libraries.description: A list of .vmodule modeling libraries to assign to the simulation node.

tcp-ip-stack.description: Simulation node configuration for the TCP/IP stack.

full.title: System variables
full.description: Describes a symbol mapping file (.vmap). Please ensure that the mappings are valid for the environment. No symbol validation will be performed.

full.title: System variables
full.description: Describes a system variable file (.vsysvar/.xml).

full.title: User files
full.description: Describes a user file that can be read/written by CAPL/.NET scripts.

file-path.description: Absolute or relative path to a user file. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

name.description: A C-identifier for this variable (case sensitive).

from-environment.description: Takes the value from a system environment variable.

full.title: Variable
full.description: This variable can be used in any other node and overwritten by a scenario.
full.$comment: has to be split, since the type determines the valid values

value.description: Variable of implicit type (string, number or bool)

options.description: List of possible values for the variable
List of possible values for the variable

value.description: Variable of type string or path

options.description: List of possible values for the variable
List of possible values for the variable
List of possible values for the variable

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a VIO System configuration file (.viocfg). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Absolute or relative path to a VT System configuration file (.vtcfg). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: XCP files
full.description: Describes a XCP configuration file (.xcp.yaml/.xcpcfg).
Absolute or relative path to system variable activation file (.vsvactivation.yaml). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

Absolute or relative path to an application model (.can/.canencr/.capl/.cs/.sln/.slnx/.py/.vmodule/.dll (dotnet assembly)). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
List of defines to be passed to CAPL, .NET, Python and VCDL.

Defines the mapping of the network to an underlying layer. 
Connects the network either to simulated network ("internal-simulator"), hardware ("external-hardware") or to SIL Kit ("external-sil-kit"). 
The supported options depend on the time-source. 
"internal-realtime" and "external-software" are supporting "internal-simulator" and "external-sil-kit".
"internal-scaled" only supports "internal-simulator".
"external-hardware" supports all mappings.

full.title: CAN network settings
full.description: Settings for the CAN network.

bit-rate-k-bit-s.description:
Bit rate of the CAN bus in kbit/s. 
 If this value is set, it has priority. 
 If this value is not set, the bit rate is read from the database. If it is missing from the database, the default value is used.

full.title: CAN FD network settings
full.description: Settings for the CAN FD network.

mode.description: Operation mode setting (iso or non-iso mode).

arbitration-bit-rate-k-bit-s.description:
Bit rate for the CAN FD arbitration phase in kbit/s. 
 If this value is set, it has priority. 
 If this value is not set, the arbitration bit rate is read from the database. If it is missing from the database, the default value is used.

data-bit-rate-k-bit-s.description:
Bit rate for the CAN FD data phase in kbit/s. 
 If this value is set, it has priority. 
 If this value is not set, the data bit rate is read from the database. If it is missing from the database, the default value is used.

full.description:
Absolute or relative path to a frame logging file (.blf/.mf4/.ttl). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
.mf4 and .ttl are not supported for target architecture Linux64.

full.title: Timing conditions
full.description: Timing conditions for a replay block.

condition.description: The condition when the replay shall start.

oneOf.description: The first event of the replayed file occurs with measurement start.
The original timestamp of the replayed file is used.
The first event is delayed. Can be configured by the "delayed-ms" element.

delay-ms.description: The delay in ms if the condition "delayed" is selected. No effect otherwise.

full.title: Channel mapping
full.description: The channel mapping for a replay block.

default-mapping.description: The default mapping of a channel if not explicitly mapped.

oneOf.description: The channel is mapped to itself if there is an active network.
The channels are ignored by default.

mappings.title: Explicit mappings
mappings.description: The explicitly mapped channels. Overwrites the default mapping for the source.

anyOf.title: Map source channel
anyOf.description: Mapping from application channel of the replay file to a target network.

source-channel.description: The source application channel from the replayed file.

anyOf.title: Map source network
anyOf.description: Mapping from a named source network of the replay file to a named target network.

source-network.description: The source network name. Only works if the replay file was created by CANoe and contains the relevant metadata.

anyOf.title: Ignore source channel
anyOf.description: Ignores an application channel of the replay file.

anyOf.title: Ignore source network
anyOf.description: Ignores a named source network of the replay file.

ignore-source-network.description: The ignored source network name. Only works if the replay file was created by CANoe and contains the relevant metadata.

full.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this database in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).
Absolute or relative path to a .arxml/.dbc/.ldf/.fdepl file. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
List of defines to be passed to the VCDL importer.
Define to be passed to CAPL, .NET, Python and VCDL. The maximum length of a define's name is 128 characters.

If a diagnostic description file contains more than one ECU definition, the ECU identifier selects the one to utilize. Note that this setting is optional for diagnostic description files containing only one ECU definition.
Absolute or relative path to a diagnostic description file (.cdd/.ecd/.mdx/.pdx/.rdx). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
The variant determines which services and parameters are available for diagnostics and communication.
A diagnostic description file can contain several language versions of which one has to be selected. Note, that some diagnostic descriptions only support one single language.
The seed-and-key file is used to unlock the ECU from CAPL, test units and test modules.

full.title: Communications parameters for diagnostics
full.description: Parameters from diagnostic description for transport and diagnostic layer.

st-min-ms.description: Minimum time gap (in ms) between consecutive frames which are to be sent from the ECU to the Tester.

s3-client-ms.description:
Time (in ms) between functionally addressed TesterPresent (0x3E) request messages transmitted by the client to keep a diagnostic session other than the defaultSession active in multiple servers (functional communication) or maximum time between physically transmitted request messages to a single server (physical communication).
Alias: tester-present-time

s3-server-ms.description:
Time (in ms) for the server to keep a diagnostic session other than the defaultSession active while not receiving any diagnostic request message.
Alias: tester-present-time-ecu

p2-client-ms.description:
Timeout (in ms) for the client to wait after the successful transmission of a request message for the start of incoming response messages.
Alias: p2-max

p2-ex-client-ms.description:
Enhanced timeout (in ms) for the client to wait after the reception of a negative response message with negative response code 0x78 for the start of incoming response messages.
Alias: p2-star

p2-server-ms.description:
Maximum amount of time (in ms) for the server to start with the response message after the reception of a request message.
Alias: p2-max-ecu

p2-ex-server-ms.description:
Maximum amount of time (in ms) for the server to start with the response message after the transmission of a negative response message with negative response code 0x78 (enhanced response timing).
Alias: p2-star-ecu

p6-client-ms.description:
Timeout (in ms) for the client to wait after the successful transmission of a request message for the end of complete response messages.
Alias: p6-max

p6-ex-client-ms.description:
Enhanced timeout (in ms) for the client to wait after the reception of a negative response message with negative response code 0x78 for the end of complete response messages.
Alias: p6-star

timeout-cr-ms.description: Time the receiver waits for a frame that is due from the sender (in ms).

timeout-as-ms.description: Time the sender of a frame waits for the send confirmation (in ms).

timeout-ar-ms.description: Time for transmission of the PDU, receiver side (in ms).

timeout-bs-ms.description: Time until reception of next flow control (in ms).

time-br-ms.description: Time for next transmission of flow control (in ms).

time-cs-ms.description: Time until next transmission of consecutive frame (in ms).

full.title: CAN Settings
full.description: Settings related to diagnostics with CAN.

full.title: DoIP Settings
full.description: Settings related to diagnostics over IP.

tls-mode.description:
Behavior of the diagnostic tester for encrypted communication via TLS.
 "deactivated": The tester does not use TLS even if a security profile for TLS is configured.
 "routing-activation": The tester at first tries to set up an unencrypted TCP connection. If not successful, the tester tries to connect via TLS.
 "secure-connection-only": The tester at first tries to set up an unencrypted TCP connection. On success the tester stops communication. 
"direct-connection": The tester directly tries to connect to the ECU via TLS.

tcp-ip-stack-adapter.description: The "name" of an ip-setting or an ip-address declared in this environment, referencing a local network interface or IP address via which CANoe or a simulated tester shall communicate.

full.title: FlexRay Settings
full.description: Settings related to diagnostics of FlexRay clusters.

transport-protocol-type.description:
The following protocol types can be selected:
 "iso-10681-2"
 "autosar-2-x"
 "autosar-3-x"

tester-pdus-phys.description: List of appropriate PDUs from the database for requests and responses used by the diagnostic implementation. Only PDUs with service types "TPL", "DIAG-REQUEST", DIAG-RESPONSE" or "DIAG-STATE" are available.

tester-pdus-func.description: List of appropriate PDUs from the database for requests and responses used by the diagnostic implementation. Only PDUs with service types "TPL", "DIAG-REQUEST", DIAG-RESPONSE" or "DIAG-STATE" are available.

ecu-pdus.description: List of appropriate PDUs from the database for requests and responses used by the diagnostic implementation. Only PDUs with service types "TPL", "DIAG-REQUEST", DIAG-RESPONSE" or "DIAG-STATE" are available.

full.title: LIN Settings
full.description: Settings related to diagnostics of LIN buses.

scheduling-mode.description:
Scheduling of master request and slave response frames.
 "selected-scheduling": The currently running schedule table is used.
 "diagnostics-only": Switching between master request and slave response tables found in ldf file.
 "interleaved": Between every segment of a request or response the table, which was running before, is run once.
"direct-sending": The master request header is generated automatically, independent of the scheduling.

node-address.description: LIN node address, typically specified in the LDF file and assigned automatically with the selection of a network node.

full.title: Additional descriptions
full.description: Additional diagnostic descriptions used to extend a so called master description.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this diagnostic description in programming APIs such as CAPL.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.title: Usage of the diagnostic description
full.description: Settings to configure the tester or simulation side of the built-in diagnostic channel.

role.description:
Select the use case for the diagnostic description. 
"tester": Configuring the tester side of the built-in diagnostic channel. 
"ecu-simulation": Configuring the simulation side of the built-in diagnostic channel.

full.title: Main device
full.description: EtherCAT main device settings.

full.title: Sub device
full.description: EtherCAT sub device settings.
Via the key slot configuration it is possible to transmit two start-up/sync frames. Thus an external start-up node is not required for the start-up of a FlexRay cluster.

full.title: Channel mapping
full.description: The channel mapping for a FlexRay replay block.

default-mapping.description: The default mapping of a channel if not explicitly mapped.

oneOf.description: All channels linked to the network this node is attached to are mapped.
The channels are ignored by default.

mappings.title: Explicit mappings
mappings.description: The explicitly mapped channels. Overwrites the default mapping for the source.

anyOf.title: Map source network
anyOf.description: Mapping of a named source network from the replay file to the network this node is linked to.

source-network.description: The source network name. Only works if the replay file was created by CANoe and contains the relevant metadata.

anyOf.title: Map source channel
anyOf.description: Mapping of an application channel from the replay file to the network this node is linked to.

source-channel.description: The source application channel from the replayed file.

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a functional mockup unit file (.fmu). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Global configuration for the TCP/IP stack.

selected-stack.description: If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used. If set to "canoe", a custom configuration can be provided, which can be configured at ethernet-networks[]/global-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.
If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used. If set to "canoe", a custom configuration can be provided, which can be configured at ethernet-networks[]/global-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.

oneOf.description: If set to "canoe", a custom configuration can be provided (remove other elements for option "operating-system").

selected-stack.description:
If a linked database contains a stack configuration, it is preferred over other configurations. If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used.
 If set to "canoe", the configuration on the global level is used.
 If set to "individual", a custom configuration can be provided at ethernet-networks[]/test-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.

name.description:
The name of the test stack, used as name of the test port.
Must be a C-identifier.

selected-stack.description:
If a linked database contains a stack configuration, it is preferred over other configurations. If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used.
 If set to "canoe", the configuration on the global level is used.
 If set to "individual", a custom configuration can be provided at ethernet-networks[]/test-tcp-ip-stack-adapter.

name.description:
The name of the test stack, used as name of the test port.
Must be a C-identifier.

full.title: Global protocol identification settings for Ethernet
full.description: Global protocol identification settings for Ethernet.

autosar-pdu-multiplexing.description: Protocol identification settings for AUTOSAR PDU Multiplexing.

udp-port.description: UDP port for AUTOSAR PDU multiplexing.

tcp-port.description: TCP port for AUTOSAR PDU multiplexing.

tls-port.description: TLS port for AUTOSAR PDU multiplexing.

dtls-port.description: DTLS port for AUTOSAR PDU multiplexing.

ccs-sdp.description: Protocol identification settings for CCS SDP.

ccs-v2g.description: Protocol identification settings for CCS V2G.

udp-nm.description: Protocol identification settings for UDP NM.

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a .vmodule. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.title: Hybdrid Network Mapping
full.description: A mapping connecting a CANoe and a SIL Kit network of type CAN or Ethernet.

network.description:
Name of a CAN or Ethernet network describing a CANoe network.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

sil-kit-network.description:
Name of a CAN or Ethernet network describing a SIL Kit network.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a program (.can/.canencr/.capl/.cs/.sln/.slnx/.dll). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
List of networks this node is assigned to.

Absolute or relative path to a .vmodule file. Relative paths are resolved relative to the configuration file.
 If no modeling library is found in the working directory, it is searched in the global modeling libraries.

selected-stack.description:
If a linked database contains a stack configuration, it is preferred over other configurations. If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used.
 If set to "canoe", the configuration on the global level is used (this requires global-settings/ethernet/tcp-ip-stack/selected-stack to be set to "canoe").
 If set to "individual", a custom configuration can be provided at network-assignments[]/tcp-ip-stack-adapter.

If a linked database contains a stack configuration, it is preferred over other configurations. If set to "operating-system", the machine configuration is used.
 If set to "canoe", the configuration on the global level is used (this requires global-settings/ethernet/tcp-ip-stack/selected-stack to be set to "canoe").
 If set to "individual", a custom configuration can be provided at network-assignments[]/tcp-ip-stack-adapter.

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a symbol mapping file (.vmap). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Absolute or relative path to a system variable file (.vsysvar/.xml). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.

full.$comment: can't use 18446744073709551615 since our validator uses int64

full.description: Absolute or relative path to a XCP configuration file (.xcp.yaml/.xcpcfg). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Sample point in percent.
Absolute or relative path to a datasource file (.vcdl/.vcodm). Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Define to be passed to the VCDL importer.

req-can-id-type-phys.description: Defines if the used CAN identifier is an "11-bit" or a "29-bit" identifier. For J1939 the identifier "29-bit" is also used.

req-can-id-type-func.description: Defines if the used CAN identifier is an "11-bit" or a "29-bit" identifier for functional channels. For J1939 the identifier "29-bit" is also used.

res-can-id-type.description: Defines if the used CAN identifier is an "11-bit" or a "29-bit" identifier for diagnostic responses.

res-can-id-type-uudt.description: Defines if the used CAN identifier for UUDT response channel is an "11-bit" or a "29-bit" identifier. For J1939 the identifier "29-bit" is also used.

req-can-id-phys.description: The request CAN identifier for physical requests. The hex value not only describes the identifier but also the priority of the message. The higher the number, the lower the priority.

req-can-id-func.description: The request CAN identifier for functional requests.

res-can-id.description: The response CAN identifier for physical responses. The response for functional requests is sent via the physical path.

res-can-id-uudt.description: The response CAN identifier for physical responses received on the UUDT channel. The response for functional requests is sent via the physical path.

ecu-address-phys.description: Defines the ecu address. Messages addressed to the ecu will contain this address as target address within the message header.

ecu-address-func.description: Defines the group address for functionally addressed requests to a group of ecus sharing the same address. Functional messages addressed from the tester will contain this address as target address within the message header.

tester-address.description: Defines the tester address. Messages addressed from the Tester will contain this address as source address within the message header.

default-tx-priority.description: The priority of CAN frames used within physical request channel. The three bits priority field is used to optimize message latency for transmission onto the CAN bus. The priority field should be masked off by the receiver (ignored). The priority of any CAN message can be set from highest, 0 (000 bin), to lowest, 7 (111 bin).

normal.title: Normal addressing
normal.description: The CAN id contains complete addressing information and all 8 data bytes can be used to exchange data. Two CAN ids per control unit are required.

normal-fixed.title: Normal fixed addressing
normal-fixed.description: Sender and receiver are specified via CAN id and all 8 data bytes can be used to exchange data. Two CAN ids per control unit are required.

extended-base.title: Extended base addressing
extended-base.description: The destination address is saved in the first data byte, therefore 7 data bytes can be used to exchange data. Only one CAN id per control unit is required for the response.

base-address.description: Defines the base address of the used CAN identifier for both, the tester and ecu side. The tester or the ecu address can be defined during run-time.

extended-free.title: Extended free addressing
extended-free.description: The destination address is saved in the first data byte, therefore 7 data bytes can be used to exchange data. Only one CAN id per control unit is required for the response.

mixed.title: Mixed addressing
mixed.description: The first data byte is used to address a subnet, therefore 7 data bytes can be used to exchange data. Two CAN ids per control unit are required.

address-extension.description: Address extension transmitted with the first data byte of the message frame. The "address-extension" usually specifies a different subnet.

full.title: DoIP addressing
full.description: Addressing parameter in conformance with the DoIP protocol.

logical-ecu-address-func.description: Logical address of the ECU for functional diagnostic requests.

logical-ecu-address-phys.description: Logical address of the ECU for physical diagnostic requests.

logical-tester-address.description: Defines the tester address. Messages from the tester will contain this address as source address within the message header.

logical-gateway-address.description: The logical address of the DoIP gateway.

connection-mode.description: Activates the acknowledging and retry operation modes of the protocol.

padding-enabled.description: Enable padding the remaining space in a L-PDU, which is not occupied by the the maximum size of the C-PDU.

padding-byte.description: Defines the padding byte to fill the remaining space in a L-PDU, which is not occupied by the maximum size of a C-PDU.

iso-10681-2.title: ISO 10681-2 TP
iso-10681-2.description: This protocol can work on FlexRay frames or on AUTOSAR PDUs.

buffer-size.description: Maximum number of data bytes that can be received without an intermediate FC frame from the receiving network entity. For 0 no more FC frames shall be sent during the transmission of the segmented message.

bandwidth-control.description: Receiving performance level, i.e. the maximum bandwidth the receiver can receive, as reported to the sender.

tester-address.description: Messages addressed from the tester will contain this address as source address within the message header.

ecu-address-phys.description: Messages addressed to the ecu will contain this address as target address within the message header.

ecu-address-phys-2.description: Defines a second ecu address. In some environments, messages addressed from the ecu will contain this address as source address within the message header.

ecu-address-func.description: Defines the group address for functionally addressed requests to a group of ecus sharing the same address. Functional messages addressed from the tester will contain this address as target address within the message header.

autosar.title: AUTOSAR FlexRay TP 2.x / 3.x
autosar.description: This protocol can work on FlexRay frames or on AUTOSAR PDUs.

block-size.description: Maximum number of C-PDUs that can be received without an intermediate FC frame from the receiving network entity. For 0 no FC frames shall be sent during the transmission of the segmented message (send as fast as possible).

message-length.description:
Maximum length of a transport protocol's C-PDU. 
 "iso": Up to 4095 bytes, do not use extended frame types. 
 "iso-6": Up to 4095 bytes, payload length is limited to 6 bytes. 
 "l-16-m": Up to 2^24-1 bytes, E4 frames not allowed. 
 "l-4-g": Up to 2^32-1 bytes, all frames allowed.

tx-max-prio-pdus-phys.description: Priority of a connection used for physical requests if connections share tx slots (lower values indicate higher priority).

tx-max-prio-pdus-func.description: Priority of a connection used for functional requests if connections share tx slots (lower values indicate higher priority).

tester-address.description: Messages addressed from the tester will contain this address as source address within the message header. Depending on "address-type", the address can be 1 byte or 2 bytes in length.

tester-address-2.description: Defines an additional tester address. In some environments, messages addressed from the ecu will contain this address as target address within the message header. Depending on "address-type", the address can be 1 byte or 2 bytes in length.

ecu-address-phys.description: Messages addressed to the ecu will contain this address as target address within the message header. Depending on "address-type", the address can be 1 byte or 2 bytes in length.

ecu-address-phys-2.description: Defines a second ecu address. In some environments, messages addressed from the ecu will contain this address as source address within the message header. Depending on "address-type", the address can be 1 byte or 2 bytes in length.

ecu-address-func.description: Defines the group address for functionally addressed requests to a group of ecus sharing the same address. Functional messages addressed from the tester will contain this address as target address within the message header. Depending on "address-type", the address can be 1 byte or 2 bytes in length.

full.title: Simulation settings for the diagnostic description
full.description: Settings for the simulation side of the built-in diagnostic channel.
Absolute or relative path to an EtherCAT network information (.eni) file. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Absolute or relative path to an XML variables export (.xve) file. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
Absolute or relative path to an extended EtherCAT subDevice information (.exi) file. Relative path specifications are resolved relative to the configuration file.
MAC address of the current TCP/IP stack (cannot be a multicast address).
Maximum Transmission Unit.
Configurations for IPv4.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv4 setting in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

address-configuration.description: Defines how the addresses are configured. Use "static" to configure them manually with the "addresses" property, "dhcp" for automatic configuration or "link-local" to provide a link-local address according to RFC3927.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv4 setting in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

address-configuration.description: Defines how the addresses are configured. Use "static" to configure them manually with the "addresses" property, "dhcp" for automatic configuration or "link-local" to provide a link-local address according to RFC3927.

full.description: Configurations for IPv6.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv6 setting in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

address-configuration.description: Defines how the addresses are configured. Use "static" to configure them manually with the "addresses" property or use "dhcp" for automatic configuration.

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv6 setting in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

address-configuration.description: Defines how the addresses are configured. Use "static" to configure them manually with the "addresses" property or use "dhcp" for automatic configuration.

vlan-settings.description: Defines a VLAN with its relevant settings.

id.$comment: reason for 4094: see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_802.1Q - VLAN identifier (VID)

full.description: A list of VLANs with their relevant settings.
Configuration of a key slot node.

mode.description:
Key slot operating modes. 
"automatic": the key slot is possibly active. Any active frame in the Tx buffer will be selected for this key slot, if marked as start-up and/or sync frame. 
"deactivated": the key slot is not used. 
"manual": explicit definition of the key slot properties.  If set to "manual" the properties "usage", "slot", "mask" and "leading-cold-start" are mandatory.

usage.description: The frame in this slot can be send as start-up or only as sync frame. Only allowed if property "mode" is set to "manual".

slot.description: Explicit slot number of the static segment that will be used as the key slot. Only allowed if property "mode" is set to "manual".

mask.description: Explicit channel mask used for the key slot. Only allowed if property "mode" is set to "manual".

leading-cold-start.description: If deactivated the key slot can only act as a following start-up or integrating sync frame. Only allowed if property "mode" is set to "manual".

full.description: Activates TCP Routing
Activates the TCP Delayed Ack Option.
IPv4-Address of the Gateway
IPv6-Address of the Gateway

full.title: Port configuration.
full.description: Port configuration.

network.description: Name of the Ethernet network where the port is configured.

vlan-id.description: VLAN ID to be used for packets on the port. Per default no VLAN is used.

vlan-in-clear.description: If set to "true", the VLAN tag is located before the SecTag in the packet. If set to "false", the VLAN tag is located after the SecTag, in the payload of the MACsec packet.

implementation.description:
Defines where the encoding/decoding of the MACsec frames takes place.
"prefer-hardware": The hardware implementation is automatically executed if the measurement port is assigned to a hardware channel that supports MACsec encryption in hardware.
"use-software": The software implementation is always used.
"use-hardware": If the hardware channel does not support MACsec encryption in hardware, then an error message is output and MACsec is disabled for the port.

port-configuration.description: Name of an available MACsec port configuration from the security profile.

full.description: Assigns the simulation node to a network declared in the configuration file. For an Ethernet network a TCP/IP stack can be provided.

network.description: Assigns the simulation node to a network declared in the configuration file.

database-node.description: Overwrite the database node name set at the simulation-node level. Only needed for gateway nodes when more than one database assigned to the network contains the node. To specify a database, use the syntax "dbName::nodeName".

tcp-ip-stack-adapter.description: Address configuration for the associated tcp-ip-stack. Is overwritten by a configuration from a linked database.

ipv4-subnet-mask.description: Subnet mask of an IPv4 Address

ipv4-subnet-prefix-length.description: Subnet prefix of an IPv4 Address

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv4 address in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv4 address in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.description: List of IPv4 addresses with their subnet definition
List of IPv6 addresses with their subnet definition

name.description:
A unique name that can be used to reference this IPv6 address in the configuration file.
Must be a C-identifier (case sensitive).

full.description: IPv4 address
IPv6 address
Bypass filter for Ethernet frames that should exceptionally bypass MACsec. It filters either by VLAN ID, EtherType, source MAC address or destination MAC address.

vlan-id.description: VLAN ID for which the MACsec operations should be bypassed.

ether-type.description: Value of the EtherType field for which the MACsec operations should be bypassed.

source-mac.description: Source MAC address for which the MACsec operations should be bypassed.

destination-mac.description: Destination MAC address for which the MACsec operations should be bypassed.

full.description: Defines for which direction the filter should be active.
